div.head: 245. Editorial Note
div.p: On July 26, Secretary of State Dulles arrived in Bonn on his way to a meeting of the Baghdad Pact at London July 28–31. During a 5-hour visit in the capital, he discussed with Chancellor Adenauer the situation in France and the Middle East, a possible summit meeting, and disarmament. Following the meeting with Adenauer, Dulles met with Foreign Minister Brentano to consider nuclear weapons for France, French-German relations, and a meeting of the Heads of Government of the United States, the United Kingdom, France, and the Soviet Union.
Memoranda of these conversations and related documentation are in Department of State, Conference Files: Lot 63 D123, CF 1060 and 1064.
